title: VR Testing Automation
overview: Design an automated framework for Virtual Reality applications' testing, exploration, and navigation
fullDescription:
[Virtual Reality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Virtual_reality) (VR) is a technology that creates a computer-generated 3D environment where users feel as if they are inside the scene. Through immersive interaction, users can look around, move within the space, and interact naturally with virtual objects in real time. 

Testing in VR is mostly done manually, with testers physically exploring the virtual environment and interacting with objects to check whether the system behaves as expected. This project proposes a novel framework that supports automated testing in VR, focusing on designing ongoing, reasonable interactions and navigation actions while exploring the virtual environment.

Since our goal is to develop a general solution that can be applied across different VR platforms, we leverage techniques such as computer vision and large language models (LLMs) to describe what users see in the 3D world, both visually and textually. This project is currently under active development, and we are continuing to build and refine the framework.
